name: Photography Editing Workflow Guide - Why & What to Edit

step.name: Import and organise your files
step.text: Set up a Year/yyyy-mm-dd folder structure, apply standard copyright metadata as an import preset, and render 1:1 previews to speed up culling. Let your catalogue file images by date automatically.

step.name: Select and cull your shortlist
step.text: Use a star-rating ladder across multiple passes — reject (X), 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 stars — narrowing to keepers. Apply colour labels for workflow state: Green to edit, Yellow bracketed, Red to delete, Blue published, Purple portfolio.

step.name: Apply global adjustments
step.text: Work the basic panel top-to-bottom: lens correction, crop and straighten, white balance, exposure, contrast, highlights and shadows, whites and blacks, texture/clarity/dehaze, vibrance and saturation. Use Alt or Option to see clipping masks.

step.name: Refine tonal character
step.text: Use tone curve, HSL, and colour grading to shape mood. Apply gentle S-curves for depth, HSL for individual colour-range control, colour grading for cinematic shadow/highlight tinting. Restraint over heaviness.

step.name: Make local adjustments
step.text: Use spot removal, adjustment brushes, gradients, AI masks, and plug-ins to support the intent set in Stage 3. Every selective adjustment must answer to the editing brief.

step.name: Export with intent
step.text: Generate exports for each destination from the master file: sRGB JPEG for web, lab-specified colour space and PPI for print, full-resolution for client delivery. Apply output sharpening appropriate to the destination medium.
